candidate_problems.text: What precise, non-ad hoc principle determines 'gravitational legibility'—i.e., which components of the stress-energy/vacuum sector gravitate and which are projected out—while preserving general covariance, local energy-momentum conservation, and the empirical successes of general relativity?

Can an information-selective coupling be formulated as a consistent theory compatible with quantum field theory and semiclassical gravity, especially in situations where vacuum structure appears to gravitate indirectly (Casimir effect, phase transitions, inflation, Hawking/Unruh phenomena)?

What unique observational signatures would distinguish this residual-projection account of dark energy from an ordinary cosmological constant or other modified-gravity models, given that the conjecture predicts near-perfect smoothness and constancy?
